title: 6 Exchanging Files on the File and Report Exchange Online via the Client Centre
body_text:
6	Exchanging Files on the File and Report Exchange Online via the Client Centre
6.1	Overview of Exchanging Files on the File and Report Exchange Online via the Client Centre
This Chapter describes how to exchange files on the File and Report Exchange online via the Client Centre. This connection option isn芒聙聶t compatible with automated scripting processes.
Once users log in to the File and Report Exchange online via the Client Centre, they only see the folders to which they have authorized access.
Note:	Business member users who require online access to the File and Report Exchange must use the procedures in Accessing the File and Report Exchange Online via the Client Centre 芒聙聯 Business Member Procedures, Form 3430.
6.2	Accessing the File and Report Exchange Online via the Client Centre
To log in to the File and Report Exchange online via the Client Centre:
Go to
https://clients.central1.com
. Enter your username and password, then select
Sign in
. See Figure 1.
Note: 	You芒聙聶ll be prompted to enter your username before this page appears if it芒聙聶s your first login to the Client Centre, you芒聙聶ve deleted your cookies, or you芒聙聶re using incognito/private browsing.
Figure 1: Client Centre Login
Select
Applications
. See Figure 2.
Figure 2: Accessing Applications on the Client Centre
Select
File and Report Exchange
. See Figure 3.
Note: 	Applications are grouped by type (e.g., Admin & Support, Payments). If needed, scroll down the page to find the application. If you芒聙聶ve saved the application as a favourite, it will also appear under 芒聙聹My Favourites芒聙聺.
Figure 3: Accessing File and Report Exchange
View the folders that you are authorized to access. See Figure 4.
Figure 4: Main Page
6.3 	Uploading Files to the File and Report Exchange Online via the Client Centre
To upload a file to the File and Report Exchange online via the Client Centre:
Log in to the File and Report Exchange as described in Section 6.2, Accessing the File and Report Exchange Online via the Client Centre.
Open the folder to which the file will be uploaded.
Important
:	Ensure that the file is uploaded to the correct folder. For example, AFT files must be uploaded to the AFT folder. If the file isn芒聙聶t uploaded to the correct folder, Central 1 will not process the file. Information on uploading specific files, including the folder name, is provided in the procedures on the relevant product or service. See the appropriate Part in the Payment Solutions Manual.
Select
Upload
. See Figure 5.
Figure 5: File Upload
Select
Browse
. See Figure 6.
Figure 6: Browse for File
Select the appropriate file, then select
Upload
.
Close the Upload screen when the file is uploaded.
Important
:	Ensure that the file is successfully uploaded. Some file types are validated. If applicable, ensure that a confirmation message or file is provided. This doesn芒聙聶t apply to all files that are uploaded to Central 1. See the Payment Solutions Manual for procedures on uploading files for specific products or services.
Select
Parent Directory
or
Home
to return to the main page. See Figure 7.
Figure 7: Return to Main Page
6.4	Downloading Files from the File and Report Exchange Online via the Client Centre
To download a report or data file from the File and Report Exchange online via the Client Centre:
Log in to the File and Report Exchange as described in Section 6.2, Accessing the File and Report Exchange Online via the Client Centre.
Open the folder containing the file you want to download. Some files may be saved in the root directory rather than a folder. These files are visible from the main page.
Note:	Select a column heading to sort the files by column.
Click once on the file to highlight it, then select
Download
. Alternatively, double-click on the file. See Figure 8.
Figure 8: Download File
Save the file to an internal location. Files are available on the File and Report Exchange for 30 calendar days. The reports from the CRF* and ICR1 files will be purged after seven calendar days since these are advance versions of existing Canadian AFT clearing reports. It is a good practice to download and save files daily.
Select
Parent Directory
or
Home
(if applicable) to return to the main page. See Figure 9.
Figure 9: Return to Main Page
END
